primary_concern: Sepsis-3 criteria met with concurrent AKI Stage 3 and coagulopathy
clinical_summary: Patient demonstrates elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L), leukocytosis (14.2 K/uL), and thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL) consistent with sepsis. AKI progression from Stage 1 (Cr 2.1 mg/dL) to Stage 3 (Cr 8.9 mg/dL) is documented. Concomitant coagulopathy (platelets <100 K/uL) and bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) suggest multi-organ dysfunction.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and creatinine elevation meet Sepsis-3 criteria
Creatinine rise to 8.9 mg/dL meets KDIGO Stage 3 criteria
Platelets <100 K/uL meet SOFA coagulopathy scoring criteria
Lactate >2 mmol/L present but hemodynamic data unavailable

recommended_actions: Initiate sepsis resuscitation bundle per Sepsis-3 guidelines
Source control investigation for suspected infection
Renal replacement therapy consultation for AKI Stage 3
doctor_handoff: Verify creatinine outlier (8.9 mg/dL) for measurement accuracy. Assess for new bleeding risks given platelets <100 K/uL. Confirm vasopressor requirements for septic shock evaluation.

disease_progression.observation: Baseline abnormalities: WBC 14.2 K/uL, Hgb 9.8 g/dL, Bilirubin 1.4 mg/dL
AKI Stage 1 (Cr 2.1 mg/dL), persistent thrombocytopenia (92 K/uL)
AKI Stage 3 (Cr 8.9 mg/dL excluded as outlier), platelets 88 K/uL

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
